info.title: Varify.io API Documentation
info.description:
The Varify.io Public API allows you to programmatically manage A/B testing experiments, retrieve analytics reports, and integrate Varify with your existing workflows.

**Base URL:** `https://app.varify.io/papi` | **Rate Limit:** 60 requests/minute

⚠️ **To get started**, see the **Authentication** section below to obtain an access token.

**Need Help?** Contact us at support@varify.io

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Endpoints for obtaining and managing API access tokens.

tags.name: Schema
tags.description: JSON Schema definitions for complex request fields

default.description: Use the **Authentication → Get Access Token** endpoint to obtain your bearer token.

post.description:
Exchange your Client ID and Client Secret for an Access Token using OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow.

**Important:** You must use this endpoint first before calling any other API endpoints. The Client ID and Client Secret cannot be used directly for API authentication.

The returned `access_token` should be included in the `Authorization` header of all subsequent API requests.

expires_in.description: Token validity duration in seconds (default 1 year).

access_token.description: The JWT access token to use in the Authorization header.

grant_type.description: The OAuth grant type. Must be `client_credentials`.

client_id.description: Your API Client ID from Team Settings → API Keys.

client_secret.description: Your API Client Secret (shown only once when created).

scope.description: The scope of access. Use `*` for full access, or specific scopes like `client:index, experiment:index, experiment:show, experiment:create, experiment:update, experiment:report`.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of all clients belonging to your agency.

**Required scope:** `client:index`

**Note:** This endpoint is only available for agency accounts.

Returns a paginated list of experiments for the specified client.

**Required scope:** `experiment:index`

post.description:
Creates a new A/B testing experiment with the specified configuration.

**Required scope:** `experiment:create`

The experiment will be created in **draft** status. Use the "Update experiment status" endpoint to activate it.

url.description: URL of the page where experiment runs

get.description:
Returns detailed information about a specific experiment including variations and targeting.

**Required scope:** `experiment:show`

patch.description:
Updates an existing experiment's configuration, variations, and targeting rules.

**Required scope:** `experiment:update`

You can update any combination of fields - only the provided fields will be modified.

**Variations:** To update existing variations, include their `id`.
To create new variations, omit the `id` field.

post.description:
Changes the status of an experiment.

**Required scope:** `experiment:update-status`

**Available statuses:**
- `draft` - Experiment is not running, can be edited freely
- `active` - Experiment is running and collecting data
- `paused` - Experiment is temporarily stopped
- `completed` - Experiment has finished running
- `archived` - Experiment is archived and hidden from default views

Creates a copy of an existing experiment including all its variations, targeting rules, and configuration.

The duplicated experiment will be created in **draft** status regardless of the original experiment's status.

**Required scope:** `experiment:duplicate`

parameters.name: experimentId
parameters.description: The ID of the experiment to duplicate

post.description:
Updates the traffic allocation percentages for an experiment's variations.

The total allocation across all variations must not exceed 100%.

**Required scope:** `experiment:update-traffic-allocation`

Toggles the tracking status of an experiment between enabled and disabled.

When tracking is disabled, the experiment will still run but no data will be collected.

**Required scope:** `experiment:update-tracking`

get.description:
Retrieves aggregated report data for an experiment within a specified date range.

Returns statistical data including visitors, conversions, revenue,
and statistical significance for each variation.

**Required scope:** `experiment:report`

raw_data.description: Return raw data instead of formatted

get.description:
Retrieves day-by-day report data for an experiment within a specified date range.

Returns daily breakdown of visitors, conversions, and revenue for each variation, useful for trend analysis.

**Required scope:** `experiment:report`

Retrieves raw revenue data for an experiment within a specified date range.

Returns detailed revenue metrics for each variation, useful for financial analysis and custom reporting.

**Required scope:** `experiment:report`

raw_data.description: Return raw data instead of formatted

get.description:
Returns all possible examples for audience targeting.
Each example shows a valid combination you can copy and use.

**Structure:** `[[rule1, rule2], [rule3]]` = (rule1 AND rule2) OR rule3

Returns all possible examples for page targeting. Each example shows a valid combination you can copy and use.

**Structure:** `[[rule1, rule2], [rule3]]` = (rule1 AND rule2) OR rule3
